CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-10-2006 Off Calendar

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-02-2006 Continued To Apr-18-06 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 212

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-17-2006 Assigned To Trial Court

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Jan-17-2006 Case Settled - Set For Crc 525 Hearing On Mar-07-06 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 212
MINI MINUTES: This matter came regularly on calendar this day, by assignment from the Department of the Presiding Judge for Court Trial Settlement on the Record.
Dept. 218, Comm. Sue Kaplan, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: Yvonne Lee.
Josepy Tursi, Esq. for for Plaintiff: Bradford-Hamilton.
Plaintiff's Witness: William Hamilton, also present.
Defendant: Thomas J. Lalanne, present in pro per.
Parties settled, terms as follows:
Defendant shall pay to Plaintiff the sum of $5,500.00 by C/C, to be mailed to Counsel by Thursday, Plaintiff will file a dismissal there after.
Parties understand and agreed to terms.
Matter is deemed settled, trial vacated, and placed on the CRC-225 Calendar in Dept. 212 for Dimissal on March 7, 2006 at 10:00 A.M.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Response To Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanction
Off Calendar Dec-16-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED.
Plaintiff's motion to compel responses to form interrogatories is granted. The motion is not moot as the answers served after the filing of the motion are incomplete. Defendant is ordered to serve complete verified responses without objection within 15 days of notice of this order. Failure to respond in a timely fashion was a misuse of the discovery process. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $546.30 are to be paid within 15 days of notice of this order. Prevailing party to submit form of order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-06-2005 Set For Trial On Jan-17-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 206
